title: The Boat That Rocked
plot: This movie is an ensemble comedy in which the romance takes place between the young people of the 1960s and pop music. It's about a band of rogue DJs that captivated Britain, playing the music that defined a generation and standing up to a government that wanted classical music, and nothing else, on the airwaves. The Count (Philip Seymour Hoffman), a big, brash, American god of the airwaves; Quentin (Bill Nighy), the boss of Radio Rock - a pirate radio station in the middle of the North Sea that's populated by an eclectic crew of rock and roll DJs; Gavin (Rhys Ifans), the greatest DJ in Britain who has just returned from his drug tour of America to reclaim his rightful position; Dave (Nick Frost), an ironic, intelligent, and cruelly funny co-broadcaster; and a fearsome British government official out for blood against the drug takers and lawbreakers of a once-great nation.
actors: Philip Seymour Hoffman, Bill Nighy, Nick Frost
production_country: United Kingdom, France, Germany, United States

categories.label: offshore ships slipping past national law
categories.description: Waters beyond the coast offer a narrow space where entrepreneurs can evade licensing and operate just outside ordinary reach. What you'll see: rusting vessels at anchor, cramped cabins turned into studios, supply runs from shore, and weather as a constant hazard.

categories.label: censorship fought through moral language
categories.description: Efforts to curb new entertainment are often framed as a defence of decency, order, and the nation's standards rather than as naked repression. What you'll see: condemnations of vulgarity, official concern about youth behaviour, speeches about standards, and respectable opinion set against pleasure.
